briefs.title: Council to Consider Appointment of Chief Election Officer for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider appointing a Chief Election Officer for the 2026 General Local Election. The Chief Election Officer is in charge of running the election. Council is expected to vote on this appointment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body: 7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER 1. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Renewal of North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA)
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider renewing the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. Council received a report on the petitions for and against the renewal. A BIA is an organization of local businesses that works to improve a specific commercial area.
briefs.source_body: 7.2 CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL 1. Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period. see Bylaw #14797 Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_ Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf

briefs.title: Council to Approve Burnaby Housing Authority Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving some yearly corporate tasks for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA). The BHA provides affordable housing in Burnaby. These tasks are things the BHA needs to do as a corporation. Council's approval is required for these.
briefs.source_body: 7.3 BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026 1. BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA). BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 Finances and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) financial results for 2025 and its budget for 2026. The BHA manages affordable housing in Burnaby. Council will review the BHA's financial statements and planned spending for the coming year.
briefs.source_body: 7.4 BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026 OPERATING BUDGET 1. BHA - 2026 Financial Statements 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - BHA - 2025 Audited Financial Statements 2026-04-14 .pdf 3. Attachment 2 - BHA - 2026 Operating Budget 2026-04-14 .pdf Purpose: To present the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget. BHA - 2026 Financial Statements 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - BHA - 2025 Audited Financial Statements 2026-04-14 .pdf Attachment 2 - BHA - 2026 Operating Budget 2026-04-14 .pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter Bylaw Amendments
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's Street and Traffic Bylaw and Parks Regulation Bylaw. The proposed changes would allow for an E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address rules for the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program. These programs would set rules for where and how these devices can be used in Burnaby. The bylaw defines how the rules will be enforced.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the final bylaw changes to ensure compliance.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM 1. Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City. Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Pr Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider sending a report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This report shows Burnaby's progress toward meeting housing targets set by the province. The Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation require this report.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 7.6 PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT 1. Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report - 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Provincial Housing Target Order 2026-04-14.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Housing Target Progress Report Form 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To present the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation. Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report - 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Provincial Housing Target Order 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 2 - Housing Target Progress Report Form 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider Building Benchmark BC Participation
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider participating in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program. BBBC is a voluntary program that helps building owners and managers track and improve the energy and environmental performance of their buildings. The program provides tools and resources to measure energy use, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and save money.
briefs.action_required: Consider how your building's energy use compares to others in BC.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Hotel & Accommodation
briefs.source_body: 7.7 BUILDING BENCHMARK BC 1. Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Building Benchmark BC Annual Report 2024 (Year 5) 2026-04-14 .pdf Purpose: To update Council on Building Benchmark BC and seek authorization to participate in Year 7. Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Building Benchmark BC Annual Report 2024 (Year 5) 2026-04-14 .pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: FortisBC Right-of-Way on Pandora St and Gaglardi Way
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider granting FortisBC a Statutory Right-of-Way (SRW) on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. An SRW gives FortisBC the right to use that land to install, operate, and maintain natural gas equipment. Council will vote on whether to approve this SRW at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision if your business operates at or near 7950 Pandora St or 1200 Gaglardi Way.
briefs.source_body: 7.8 7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY - BURNABY MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY FOR FORTIS BC 1. 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way - Burnaby Mountain Fortis SRW 2026-04-14 .pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works. 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way - Burnaby Mountain Fortis SRW 2026-04-14 Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract for New Modular Housing Four-Plex in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for a pilot project: the development of a modular four-plex housing unit. This project would be located at 8315 14th Avenue. Council is set to vote on whether to approve this contract.
briefs.source_body: 7.9 CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX AT 8315 14TH AVENUE 1. CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project . CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract for Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The project aims to replace the existing culvert, which is a structure that allows water to flow under a road or railway. Council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Monitor council's decision, as construction projects can impact local businesses.
briefs.source_body: 7.10 CA - (CB-8039) JERRY ROGERS CREEK CULVERT REPLACEMENT 1. CA - CB-8039 Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. CA - CB-8039 Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract for Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. This project involves replacing a culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream under a road or path) and paving work along the Greenway. Council's decision is up for approval at the next meeting.
briefs.source_body: 7.11 CA - (CB-3264) CENTRAL VALLEY GREENWAY CULVERT REPLACEMENT AND PAVING WORKS 1. CA - (CB-3264) Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. CA - (CB-3264) Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract Change for Cellular Voice and Data Services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the contract for cellular voice and data services. This contract covers the City's cell phone and data plans. Council will vote on whether to approve this change at an upcoming meeting. The details of the proposed changes are in the linked report.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Change to HVAC Maintenance Contract for City Buildings
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the contract for maintaining and repairing HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) systems in city-owned buildings. Council will vote on whether to approve this contract change at an upcoming meeting. The purpose is to ensure these systems are properly maintained.
briefs.action_required: No action required. Monitor City Council decisions if you operate in HVAC maintenance and repair.

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Renewal of Cisco WebEx Contract Center License
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider renewing the contract for the Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription. This subscription allows the city to use Cisco WebEx for its contact center operations. Council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting.

briefs.title: Council to Vote On: Fire Protection System Maintenance Contract Renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider renewing a contract for fire protection systems and equipment maintenance. The contract covers installation, maintenance, and repair services. Council will vote on whether to approve the contract renewal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to Approve Plaque for Burnaby Lake Overpass Opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving the wording and content of a commemorative plaque. The plaque will be installed for the opening of the Burnaby Lake Overpass. Council is set to vote on the plaque's design at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body: 7.16 BURNABY LAKE OVERPASS OFFICIAL OPENING PLAQUE 1. Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for the wording and content for the commemorative plaque to be installed as part of the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass. Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider External Review of City Finances
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider hiring an outside expert to review the City's financial practices. This review aims to protect the City's money and financial health. It also seeks to lower the risk of big tax increases and rising costs for local businesses and residents. The goal is to ensure the City is managing its finances responsibly.
briefs.action_required: No action required at this time. Watch for updates on this proposal.
briefs.source_body: 8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES 1. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: 20-Storey Apartment Rezoning at 5035 Beresford St.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a rezoning application for 5035 Beresford Street. The proposal is to build a 20-storey apartment building. Council is set to vote on forwarding the application to First and Second Reading at a future meeting. This is part of the Royal Oak Community Plan.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Security Forfeit for Riverbend Business Park Pier
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a developer's request to forfeit the security deposit for a public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court. The developer has chosen not to build the pier, which was part of the Riverbend Business Park rezoning agreement. The City would hold the money and decide later how to use it.
briefs.source_body: 9.2 AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY 1. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area bylaw.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a bylaw to create the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA). A BIA is an organization of local businesses that work together to improve their commercial area. However, the city has received enough petitions against the proposed BIA.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision regarding the Burnaby North Road BIA.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance
briefs.source_body: 11.1.1 #14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026 1. 14797 Burnaby BIA (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026 (FA).pdf Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association (Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026) see Item 7.2. NOTE: sufficient petitions against the proposed BIA have been received 14797 Burnaby BIA (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026 (FA).pdf
